name: Protocol compliance
definition: Verification that data transmission signals conform to the required protocol communication standards.
purpose: Ensures signal quality and compatibility with communication interface standards.
chars: (eye diagram / jitter / BER — Bit Error Rate)
delivery: Test procedure + measurement report

name: SPICE
definition: A set of mathematical equations and empirical parameters describing the electrical dynamic behavior of fundamental semiconductor devices (Transistor, Diode, Resistor, Capacitor) under varying operating conditions, expressed in netlist format.
purpose: Enables accurate simulation of analog, RF, and logic circuits.
chars:
Hundreds of parameters, but key ones include:
Core DC (static parameters): Basic threshold voltage (Vth), carrier mobility (µ), channel resistance (Rds).
RF network parameters.
AC analysis: Noise + Linearity.
Process Variation (mismatch) parameters.
Parasitic effects: Calculation of parasitic capacitances and resistances based on device geometry.
delivery: SPICE model file + model documentation

name: Statistic model
definition: The same transistor design may exhibit different performance characteristics when manufactured using different equipment, at different locations, or at different times. A statistical model is required to capture these performance variations.
purpose: Used for circuit simulation under the influence of random process variations (Monte Carlo analysis).
chars: Example: the variance of the NMOS data, the variance of the PMOS data, and the covariance of the NMOS data vs. the PMOS data.
delivery:
Statistical model file
(A .lib file or wrapper file containing statistical distribution functions.)
comment: https://gf180mcu-pdk.readthedocs.io/en/latest/analog/model_parameters/HV/HV_4.html

name: Corner model
definition: A set of models representing extreme technology conditions (fast, slow, typical) across Process, Voltage, and Temperature (PVT) variations.
purpose: Verifies that the circuit operates correctly across all PVT corner conditions.
chars: Process corners for a circuit relate to the sensitivity of the circuit design to process parameters, particularly in analog circuits. Corner models are provided primarily to predict static logic timing delay. Corner models for these devices are based entirely on on-line silicon data and technical specifications.
delivery: PVT corner model files
comment: https://gf180mcu-pdk.readthedocs.io/en/latest/analog/model_parameters/HV/HV_2_4.html#corner-model-definitions

name: Behavior model ( Verilog-A)
definition: A behavioral model of an analog circuit described using the Verilog-A hardware description language.
purpose: Used to simulate the functional behavior of an analog circuit at the behavioral abstraction level. Accelerates simulation speed for complex systems that are difficult to describe with SPICE models. An enabling technology for analog and mixed-signal top-down design. A compact and clear expression of design intent. Standardized form of communication of design information.
chars:
Uses differential equations, transfer functions, or algorithms to describe behavior. Considered an extension of SPICE.
Primarily simulates the behavioral response of analog circuits:
Constitutive relationships: Directly maps input signals to output signals using linear and nonlinear algebraic or differential functions.
delivery: Verilog-A file (.va)
comment: Reference: ANALOG BEHAVIORAL MODELING WITH THE VERILOG-A LANGUAGE

name: BEOL model (Back end of line model)
definition: A model describing the resistance, capacitance, and parasitic effects of the metal interconnect system (Back-End-Of-Line wiring stack).
purpose: Simulates the impact of interconnect wiring on signal delay and crosstalk noise.
chars: Metal layer thickness, dielectric constant (k), spacing between conductors.
delivery:
Parasitic/interconnect model file;
Opened with EDA tools, included in the PDK package.

name: EDA process tech file
definition: A file describing the technology information for EDA (Electronic Design Automation) tools, including layer definitions, design grid, and electrical connectivity rules.
purpose: Enables design tools to perform correct layout, route signals, and generate mask data.
chars: Layer definitions (layer map), units of measurement, display rules, electrical connectivity rules.
delivery: Technology file for EDA tools

name: Netlist view
definition: Describes the circuit components and their electrical connections in netlist format.
purpose: Used for circuit simulation and Layout vs. Schematic (LVS) verification.
chars:
Topology: Describes how components are interconnected through nodes.
Design hierarchy: Describes parent-child relationships between modules and lower-level building blocks.
Functional verification: Gate-level netlist is used to verify that the design meets functional specifications.
delivery:
Netlist file (CDL/SPICE format);
Viewed with Netlist viewer tool.

name: Pcell (parameterized cell)
definition: Cells that can be automatically generated based on user-defined design parameters such as transistor width (W) and length (L).
purpose: Standardizes layout generation and improves design efficiency. Automates layout drawing — instead of manual drawing, the designer simply inputs width (W) and length (L) parameters.
chars: Contains code (typically SKILL or Python scripting) to programmatically construct device geometry based on input variables.
delivery:
PCell library;
Opened with EDA tools, included in the PDK package.

name: DRC (Design rule check)
definition: A set of design rules provided to customers to ensure layout compatibility with the fab's manufacturing capability.
purpose: Checks whether geometric shapes in the layout violate minimum spacing, wire width, and other fab-specified design constraints. DRC errors prevent chip manufacturing. These rules are implemented as code segments describing logical operations on physical layers.
chars:
Rules at transistor geometry level:
I. Critical Dimensions
II. Spacing Rules
III. Overlapping Rules
IV. Density Rules
V. Layer-Specific Rules
VI. Power and Ground Rules
VII. Dummy Fill Rules
delivery: Rule Decks (DRC deck files)

name: LVS (Layout vs. Schematic)
definition: A set of rules for verifying circuit topology — checking that transistor and block connections in the layout match the original schematic.
purpose: Ensures the drawn circuit layout matches the original logic design intent. Detects incorrect signal wire shorts or missing wire connections (opens).
chars: Compares component count (Transistor, Resistor, Capacitor), cross-references net names, I/O ports, and connection types. Criterion is 'LVS Clean' (zero discrepancies).
delivery: Rule Decks / LVS tool

name: LPE (Layout Parameter Extraction)
definition: A set of rules for extracting actual physical parameters from layout drawings. Specifies how a given layer geometry and distribution creates resistance and capacitance values. Commonly referred to as the fab's parameter table — does not guarantee that the extracted design will operate at full performance.
purpose: LPE is the process of extracting actual physical parameters from layout drawings to create a detailed parasitic netlist. Unlike the ideal netlist from schematic, the LPE-extracted netlist includes parasitic components (R, C, L). Used for accurate post-layout simulation.
chars:
Extraction Rules: Rule files (typically in .lvs format or tool files such as Calibre xRC, StarRC) describing the electrical characteristics of each material layer.
Parasitic Models: Mathematical models for calculating R, L, C based on width, length, and spacing between conductors.
Technology Files: Contains process-specific parasitic parameters.
delivery: Extraction Rules / tool

name: ERC (Electrical rule check)
definition: A set of rules for checking invalid electrical connection errors in the layout design.
purpose:
Prevents shorts: Detects power (VDD) and ground (VSS) rails shorted together.
Detects opens/floating nodes: Identifies transistor gate ports or conductors unconnected to any net (floating nodes).
Voltage protection: Checks whether transistors are connected to voltages exceeding their operating specifications.
chars:
ERC Rule Deck: A collection of logical conditions (run using Calibre or Pegasus tools) to scan the entire layout.
Power/Ground Definitions: List of net names designated as power and ground for system recognition.
Soft Connect Rules: Rules checking 'soft' connections through diffusion regions or wells.
delivery: ERC rule deck files

name: PEX (Parasitic Extraction)
definition: A set of rules for extracting unwanted parasitic elements (resistance R, capacitance C, inductance L) arising from wires and physical structures. Equivalent to LPE combined with tool-specific algorithms (typically Calibre xRC).
purpose: Used for post-layout simulation. Example: Checks whether parasitic capacitance is slowing down signal propagation beyond timing specifications.
chars: 2D/3D models of coupling capacitance and wire resistance. Values expressed in femtofarads (fF) and ohms (Ω).
delivery: Rule deck file (PEX deck)

name: ESD (Electrostatic Discharge)
definition: A set of design rules for electrostatic discharge protection. ESD is the phenomenon of sudden current flow between two objects at different electrostatic potentials when they come into contact or discharge through an air gap.
purpose: Protects chip I/O circuits from damage due to electrostatic discharge events during handling, assembly, and operation.
chars:
ESD Rules: Specifications for metal width and contact/via count to withstand sudden large ESD current pulses without metal melting.
ESD Protection Cells: I/O libraries containing protection diodes, Grounded Gate NMOS (GGNMOS), or Silicon Controlled Rectifiers (SCR) to shunt ESD current to ground.
Clamp circuits: Power supply clamp cells to protect internal circuitry.
delivery: ESD rule deck + ESD protection cell library

name: Antenna
definition: The plasma-induced gate damage (antenna) effect — occurs during etch/plasma processing steps: metal conductors accumulate electrical charge from the plasma environment. If the charge cannot be discharged through a reverse-biased diode, it will discharge through the transistor gate oxide, causing permanent damage.
purpose: Protects gate oxide from plasma-induced damage, ensuring chip yield and long-term device reliability.
chars:
Antenna Ratio Rules: Maximum permissible ratio between the cumulative area of metal conductor (or polysilicon) and the gate oxide area it connects to (Metal Area / Gate Area).
Antenna Diode: Protective diodes inserted in the layout to provide a charge discharge path to the substrate during plasma processing.
Metal Jumping (layer hopping): Technique of routing through higher metal layers to break excessive charge accumulation on lower layers.
delivery: Antenna rule deck

name: Latch-up
definition: A set of design rules preventing the formation of parasitic PNPN thyristor structures between N-well and P-substrate regions in CMOS circuits.
purpose: Prevents permanent latch-up short circuits that can cause chip destruction when transient voltage spikes or noise triggers the parasitic thyristor into a self-sustaining conduction state.
chars: Specifies maximum spacing between VDD/GND tap contacts (tap-to-gate spacing) and the width of isolation regions (guard rings) required around NMOS and PMOS devices.
delivery: A dedicated chapter in the Design Rule Manual (DRM) and automated DRC check deck files for latch-up verification

name: Reliability rules
definition: Rules specifying measurement methods, measurement conditions, stress conditions, and failure criteria for device reliability verification.
purpose: Helps designs achieve the intended application reliability targets and lifetime objectives.
chars:
Gate oxide integrity (TDDB)
Hot carrier injection (HCI)
PMOS negative bias temperature instability (NBTI)
NMOS positive bias temperature instability (PBTI)
Electromigration (EM)
Low-k dielectric integrity
Stress migration (SM)
delivery: Reliability qualification specification documents
comment: TSMC reference

name: DFM
definition: Design for Manufacturability — rules and guidelines enabling designs to achieve higher model precision, better reliability, lower timing variability, and lower yield loss, under the condition that they introduce no area penalty.
purpose: Using DFM rules improves precision of parasitic models, enhances reliability, reduces timing, process, or yield variation, and minimizes potential and unnecessary yield or timing loss — without introducing additional area overhead.
chars:
Includes rules:
Antenna Rules (ratios, cumulative charge, jumpers)
Metal Fill/Density (CMP windows, gradients)
Via Redundancy/Stacking
Litho/OPC/RET recommended rules
RF Flavor Additions:
- Inductor/MIM dummy fill exclusion zones
- Low-loss interconnect routing (avoid dense fill near passive elements)
delivery: Document-Checklist / tool
comment: Includes material information and process flow guidance. DFM rules typically embedded within EDA tool rule decks.

name: IR drop rule
definition: A design rule for checking the voltage drop (IR drop) across the chip power distribution network (PDN).
purpose: Ensures transistors located far from the power source still receive sufficient voltage to operate at the correct speed and logic levels.
chars: Static and dynamic IR drop limits, typically not exceeding 2–5% of Vdd at any node in the power grid.
delivery: Power model file (e.g., .tech file for Voltus/RedHawk) and metal sheet resistance parameter table

name: EM rule (Electromigration)
definition: A design rule limiting current density in metal wires to prevent electromigration-induced conductor failure.
purpose: Ensures long-term interconnect reliability and prevents metal wire failure (open circuits) during the product's operational lifetime.
chars: Maximum allowable current density per unit cross-sectional area of conductor (mA/µm²) at various junction temperatures (e.g., 105°C, 125°C).
delivery: Electromigration verification deck + current density guideline

name: Thermal-aware rule
definition: Design guidelines that account for temperature distribution across the chip surface during normal operation.
purpose: Prevents localized thermal hotspots that can cause device failure, parametric degradation, or timing errors in nearby circuits.
chars: Specifications for maximum power density per unit area (mW/µm²) and minimum required spacing between high-power dissipation blocks.
delivery: Thermal Design Guideline document and thermal simulation model files

name: Mask data preparation interface
definition: Specifications and rules governing the preparation of mask design data for the mask fabrication interface.
purpose: Defines the required data format, layer naming conventions, and design intent information needed to submit layout data for mask manufacturing.
chars:
-Data format requirements
-Layer mapping and naming conventions
-Mask intent and special layer usage
-Feature-related constraints
-OPC/RET interface definitions
-Seal ring, scribe line, and test structure interface requirements
delivery: Interface specification document

name: Test acceess I/O guidline
definition: Design rules for the placement and design of test pads (bond pads) to enable post-manufacturing chip testing.
purpose: Ensures that automated test equipment (ATE) can access internal circuits for parametric and functional defect measurement (DFT — Design for Test).
chars:
Pad dimensions, pad pitch, and JTAG/scan-chain pinout diagram.
*Test pin definition and usage
*I/O behavior in test modes
*Electrical safety guidance
*Probe and packaging test considerations
*Boundary scan/JTAG guidance
delivery: I/O Library and Application Notes documentation

name: Timing and signal integerity
definition: Rules governing signal propagation timing accuracy and signal integrity (noise and crosstalk avoidance).
purpose: Ensures the chip operates at the designed clock frequency and is free from data errors caused by crosstalk between signal lines.
chars: Setup/hold time specifications (in ps), signal slew rate (V/ns), noise margin, and coupling capacitance limits.
delivery: Liberty timing library file (.lib) and signal integrity configuration files

name: STA-Static Timing Analysis
definition: Static Timing Analysis (STA) is a verification method that checks whether signals arrive at their destination registers within valid timing windows — without requiring dynamic circuit simulation.
purpose: Ensures the chip meets its target operating frequency (e.g., 2 GHz) regardless of small temperature or manufacturing process variations. Prevents hold-time violations that cause functional failures.
chars:
AOCV (Advanced OCV): Distance-dependent delay variation modeling.
POCV (Parametric OCV): Uses Gaussian statistical distributions for each cell delay. Metric: Sigma (σ), typically verified at 3σ.
CPPR (Common Path Pessimism Removal): Removes redundant clock path delay pessimism to tighten timing margins.
delivery: Advanced Liberty timing data file (.lib) with Liberty Variation Format (LVF) lookup tables

name: Synthesis/CTS/P&R
definition:
Synthesis (Logic Synthesis): Converts RTL source code (Verilog/VHDL) into a gate-level netlist using the fab's Standard Cell Library.
CTS (Clock Tree Synthesis): Builds the clock distribution network to deliver the clock signal simultaneously to all flip-flops (minimizing clock skew).
P&R (Place and Route): Physically places standard cells in optimized positions and routes the metal interconnections between them.
purpose: Creates a physically optimized implementation of the logic design, meeting constraints for timing, power consumption, and chip area.
chars:
Gate Count: Equivalent logic gate count.
Area: Total layout area (µm²).
Timing Slack: Remaining time margin relative to the clock period (must be ≥ 0 for setup and hold).

name: Clocking
definition: The complete system for managing and distributing clock signals throughout the entire chip, including clock generation (PLL/DLL), distribution (clock tree), and gating.
purpose: Provides a stable, low-jitter clock signal to all synchronous logic while minimizing dynamic power consumption. Example: PLL generates 3 GHz on-chip clock from an external 25 MHz crystal oscillator.
chars:
CG (Clock Gating): Technique for halting the clock to inactive circuit blocks to reduce dynamic power.
PLL/DLL (Phase-Locked Loop / Delay-Locked Loop): Circuits that generate precise high-frequency clocks from a lower-frequency reference.
Jitter: Clock signal timing uncertainty (typically required < 10 ps RMS).
Lock Time: Time required for the PLL to acquire frequency lock.
Duty Cycle: Clock duty cycle ratio.
delivery: Datasheet, Verilog/behavioral simulation model, and hard macro layout (GDSII) for PLL/DLL

name: Low-power kits (retention/isolation)
definition: Components and design rules specifically for complex power management designs with multiple voltage domains (multi-VDD).
purpose: Reduces standby leakage current and prevents undefined logic states when portions of the chip are power-gated. Example: When the CPU core domain is powered off, output signal pins must be isolated to prevent corrupting logic in other active power domains.
chars:
Retention Cell: Preserves the state of flip-flops when the main power supply is switched off.
Isolation Cell: Forces output signals to a defined safe value (0 or 1) when the input power domain is powered down.
Level Shifter: Translates signal voltage levels between two different power domains.
delivery: Power Management Kit (PMK) library; UPF (Unified Power Format) or CPF (Common Power Format) power definition files

name: Standard cell families
definition: A library of standard logic cells (NAND, NOR, AND, OR, flip-flops, etc.) available in multiple threshold voltage (Vt) and drive strength variants for performance and power optimization.
purpose: Foundation for logic synthesis and digital implementation — provides the building blocks for all synthesized logic circuits.
chars: Multiple threshold voltage flavors (HVT, SVT, LVT) and drive strength variants for timing/power trade-off.
delivery: Cell library files (GDS, LEF, Liberty .lib, Verilog netlist)

name: SRAM compilers
definition: Software tools for automatically generating custom SRAM memory array blocks with user-specified configuration (depth, width) and performance targets.
purpose: Creates memory blocks optimized for area, access speed, or power consumption without manual layout effort.
chars: (HD/HC/HS — High Density / High Capacity / High Speed variants)
delivery: SRAM compiler tool + generated memory views (GDS, LEF, Liberty .lib, Verilog model)

name: ESD clamps/pad rings
definition: Electrostatic discharge protection clamping structures and pad ring assemblies surrounding the chip periphery.
purpose: Protects the chip from damage due to electrostatic discharge and provides the mechanical and electrical interface for I/O connectivity.
chars: Power supply clamp cells, corner pad cells, guard ring structures.
delivery: ESD clamp and pad ring layout cell library

name: IO libraries
definition: A library of hardened input/output interface cells for signal communication between the chip core and the external package environment.
purpose: Ensures proper signal communication and voltage level translation between different power domains and external interfaces.
chars: (1.8 V / 2.5 V / 3.3 V variants; GPIO / LVDS / XTAL / level shifters)
delivery: IO IP library (GDS, LEF, Liberty .lib, behavioral model)

name: PCIe
definition: High-speed serial data interface IP conforming to the PCI Express standard for chip-to-chip and chip-to-peripheral communication.
purpose: Connects the chip to peripheral devices, storage subsystems, or processing units in a system-level design.
chars: PCIe PHY, controller, and verification IP.
delivery: Interface IP (GDS, LEF, model, integration guide)

name: USB
definition: Universal Serial Bus (USB) interface IP for standard device connectivity.
purpose: Connects the chip to peripheral devices and computer systems using the industry-standard USB protocol.
chars: USB PHY, controller IP.
delivery: Interface IP (GDS, LEF, model, integration guide)

name: MIPI
definition: Mobile Industry Processor Interface (MIPI) high-speed serial interface IP for camera and display applications.
purpose: Transfers image data and display information between processors and peripherals in SoC designs.
chars: (CSI — Camera Serial Interface / DSI — Display Serial Interface)
delivery: Interface IP (GDS, LEF, model, integration guide)

name: Ethernet
definition: Ethernet network communication interface IP for wired data connectivity.
purpose: Connects the chip to network infrastructure and enables high-speed wired data transmission.
chars: (SGMII / GMII — Serial/Gigabit Media Independent Interface variants)
delivery: Interface IP (GDS, LEF, model, integration guide)

name: Analog
definition: Fundamental hardened analog IP blocks for power management and on-chip monitoring functions.
purpose: Supports power supply regulation, on-chip temperature sensing, and chip operational status monitoring.
chars: (Bandgap reference, LDO — Low-DropOut regulator, temperature sensor)
delivery: Analog IP + simulation model + datasheet

name: Mixed signal
definition: Hardened conversion and signal processing IP blocks operating at the interface between analog and digital signal domains.
purpose: Enables signal conversion and synchronization between analog and digital processing domains in mixed-signal SoC designs.
chars: (ADC — Analog-to-Digital Converter, DAC — Digital-to-Analog Converter, PLL — Phase-Locked Loop, DLL — Delay-Locked Loop)
delivery: Mixed-signal IP + simulation model

name: High speed
definition: High-speed serial data link transmit and receive IP blocks.
purpose: Supports multi-gigabit per second data transmission over high-speed serial communication channels.
chars: (SER/DES — Serializer/Deserializer, CDR — Clock and Data Recovery)
delivery: High-speed SerDes IP (GDS, LEF, model, integration guide)

name: RF/mmwave
definition: Hardened high-frequency (RF / millimeter-wave) signal processing IP blocks.
purpose: Enables reception, amplification, and transmission of high-frequency signals in wireless communication and radar systems.
chars: (LNA — Low Noise Amplifier, PA — Power Amplifier, Mixer, VCO — Voltage Controlled Oscillator)
delivery: RF IP (GDS, LEF, model, datasheet)

name: prioritized RF front-ends
definition: RF front-end IP blocks optimized for specific priority wireless communication applications.
purpose: Improves RF transceiver performance and integration efficiency for targeted wireless communication applications.
chars: Pre-characterized RF front-end blocks optimized for specific frequency bands and modulation standards.
delivery: RF front-end IP + reference design

name: DFT
definition: A collection of design structures and methodologies supporting post-manufacturing chip testing, including scan chains, Built-In Self-Test (BIST), and boundary scan (JTAG).
purpose: Rapidly identifies defective chips from functional chips using Automated Test Equipment (ATE). Detects manufacturing defects such as broken connections among billions of transistors.
chars:
Scan Chain: Reconfigures flip-flops into a shift register chain for applying and capturing test patterns.
BIST (Built-In Self-Test): On-chip self-test circuits for memory (MBIST) or logic (LBIST).
Test Coverage: Defect detection rate, typically targeting ≥ 99% for single stuck-at faults.
delivery:
DFT library + test scripts;
ATPG (Automatic Test Pattern Generation) procedure and test description files (.itp, .evcd)

name: ROM
definition: Read-Only Memory (ROM) macro that stores data permanently written during the chip manufacturing process.
purpose: Stores boot firmware, hardware configuration data, or other fixed system data that does not change after manufacturing.
chars: Customized bit pattern encoded in the mask layer during manufacturing.
delivery: ROM hard macro (GDS) + memory behavioral model

name: eFuse
definition: A one-time programmable (OTP) non-volatile memory element used to permanently store unique chip-specific information by electrically blowing metal fuse links.
purpose: Stores chip identification codes, post-manufacturing memory redundancy repair data, or security keys. Example: Stores a unique serial number or device ID permanently in each mobile phone chip.
chars:
Programming Voltage: Elevated voltage required to electromigrate and blow the fuse link (typically higher than normal operating voltage).
Programming Time: Duration of programming current pulse required to permanently change fuse state (typically in the µs range).
delivery: Hard Macro (GDSII layout), simulation model, and programming sequence specification document

name: DDR/LPDDR
definition: High-speed double data rate memory interface IP for connecting the SoC to external DRAM memory devices.
purpose: Provides high memory bandwidth for high-performance computing, mobile processors, and SoC applications.
chars:
Eye Diagram: Signal quality visualization (wider eye opening indicates better signal integrity).
PHY (Physical Layer): Analog front-end handling signal conditioning and timing.
Trace Length Matching: PCB routing rules requiring equal trace lengths to prevent signal skew across the data bus.
delivery: DDR PHY + memory controller IP + integration guide

name: Device architecture
definition: Defines the physical structure of the transistor, including gate stack, source/drain regions, well structure, and isolation.
purpose: Serves as the fundamental engineering specification for manufacturing the transistor and determines its electrical performance characteristics.
chars: Geometric parameters (gate length, gate width, oxide thickness), material stack for each layer, and doping concentration profiles.
delivery: Engineering documents — cross-section drawings and device specification sheets

name: TCAD models
definition: Technology Computer-Aided Design (TCAD) process and device simulation models.
purpose: Optimizes process parameters, reduces fabrication cost and development time, predicts physical phenomena at the device level, and provides calibrated device data for PDK model development.
chars: 2D and 3D device structure simulation; process step simulation (diffusion, implant, etch, deposition); electrical characteristic simulation (I-V, C-V curves).

name: Reticle Shot Map
definition: A map defining the exposure shot positions on the reticle during the photolithography process, specifying die placement and field layout on the wafer.
purpose: Controls the spatial arrangement of die fields on the wafer to maximize usable die count while defining edge exclusion zones and special test structure placement.
chars: Shot field dimensions, die-to-die spacing, reticle field layout, scribe line widths, and wafer edge exclusion zone.
delivery: Reticle design document — shot map layout file

name: Process recipe
definition: The process recipe specifying all operating parameters for each individual process module step (lithography, etch, deposition, CMP, implant, anneal, clean, etc.) throughout the complete process flow.
purpose: Defines the exact process conditions required to fabricate each layer of the device and reproduce the target electrical and physical characteristics on the wafer.
chars: Detailed recipe parameters for each process step: gas flow rates, pressure, temperature, RF power, time, endpoint conditions, and process window limits.
delivery: Process recipe document files

name: DOE methodology
definition: A systematic Design of Experiments (DOE) methodology for structured experimental planning and statistical analysis of process parameters.
purpose: Process optimization, process robustness characterization, cost reduction through efficient experimentation, factor screening, and defect root-cause identification.
chars:
Full factorial design or fractional factorial design; response surface methodology (RSM);
Checklist of operational factors requiring DOE evaluation (typically defined as process engineering rules).
delivery: DOE documents / database
comment: Most important DOE output: parameter table with target values and acceptable operating range for each factor.

name: Material selection table
definition: Evaluation and selection of materials (process chemicals, gases, dielectrics, metals, etc.) based on their relevant physical properties (dielectric constant k, electrical conductivity, diffusivity, etc.) for integration into the manufacturing process.
purpose: Provides methodology and technical information for evaluating and selecting new process materials and process chemicals.
chars: Index tables of material properties, compatibility assessments, and process integration requirements.
delivery: Material specification documents
comment: Typically included within DFM documentation.

name: Dense device array (variability, LDE)
definition: An array of identical devices arranged at high density, designed to measure the effects of process variability and layout-dependent effects (LDE) such as well proximity effect, length of diffusion (LOD), and mechanical stress effects.
purpose: Measures the impact of systematic and random process parameter variation on device electrical characteristics. Used to build variability models for statistical design.
chars: Arrays of MOS transistors, resistors, and capacitors in various layout configurations to characterize LDE and proximity effects.
delivery: Layout test structure GDS file + extracted variability model

name: Reliability stress structure
definition: Dedicated test structures designed for accelerated reliability stress testing of devices and interconnects.
purpose: Collects electrical degradation data under accelerated stress conditions to build and validate lifetime prediction models.
chars: TDDB capacitor arrays, HCI transistor chains, EM metal stripe structures, BTI stress transistors, and SM via chain structures.
delivery: Layout test structure GDS file + stress test procedure + reliability test report

name: Lithography and parttern monitor
definition: Test structures specifically designed to monitor photolithography printability, process window margins, and to detect lithographic hotspot regions in complex pattern environments.
purpose: Monitors photolithography critical dimension (CD) control quality and supports early detection of layout patterns prone to yield failures (hotspots).
chars: CD measurement targets, pitch-varying line/space arrays, two-dimensional pattern arrays, and assist feature monitor structures.
delivery: Layout test structure GDS file + pattern monitoring library

name: Statistic limit , conner chart
definition: Statistical control charts representing the allowable variation limits of process parameters and tracking the stability of the manufacturing process over time.
purpose: Monitors process parameter variation over time and enables early detection of deviations from established statistical process control (SPC) limits.
chars: Statistical process control (SPC) charts with upper and lower control limits (UCL/LCL) for all critical process parameters.
delivery: Statistical report / SPC control charts

name: Reference structure
definition: Standard measurement reference structures used to establish and verify baseline measurements for metrology tool calibration and process parameter monitoring.
purpose: Provides a traceable reference baseline for metrology tool qualification, calibration, and long-term measurement stability verification.
chars: Calibrated dimensional standards, known-thickness reference wafers, certified overlay targets.
delivery: Reference structure GDS layout + calibration documentation

name: Measurement definitions
definition: Formal specifications defining the measurement methods, measurement sequences, sampling plans, and parameter definitions for each inline and offline measurement point in the process flow.
purpose: Ensures measurement consistency, reproducibility, and data integrity across all metrology tools and time periods.
chars: Measurement point definitions, tool assignment rules, recipe specifications, and sampling frequency guidelines for each process step.
delivery: Measurement specification documents

name: Model correlations
definition: Documented quantitative relationships between process parameters (tool settings, inline measurements) and device electrical characteristics or circuit performance metrics.
purpose: Enables model-based process control, out-of-specification event detection, validation of new process modules, and prediction of device electrical performance from process data.
chars: Correlation coefficients, transfer function models, and regression analysis linking physical/process variables to device electrical properties for each manufacturing process step.
delivery: Correlation model files + documentation

name: Integration guides
definition: Methodology documentation for establishing a complete process integration flow — the step-by-step approach to combining individual process modules into a working device.
purpose: Enables engineering teams to independently establish and adapt process flows for different device architectures and design variants.
chars: Process integration philosophy, module sequencing logic, interface requirements between modules, and decision trees for process architecture selection.
delivery: Engineering documents — process integration know-how

name: Process flow integration
definition: Process integration: establishing the ordered sequence of manufacturing steps required to fabricate a specific device structure on the wafer.
purpose: Realizes the target device architecture from design specifications, and optimizes the process module sequence to achieve yield and performance targets.
chars:
Information included:
Ordered sequence of all process steps from substrate preparation to final metal
Output specification (target values and acceptance limits) for each step
Q-time (queue time) constraints between sequential steps
delivery: Process flow document
comment: Engineering approach: Establish baseline flow → short-loop experiments → full-flow wafer runs → failure analysis (FA)

name: Process window
definition: The set of allowable operating parameter ranges within which a process step can be executed while still meeting the output specifications.
purpose: Defines and maintains stable process operation to consistently produce products meeting all required specifications.
chars: Includes upper and lower limits (specification limits and control limits) for all critical operating parameters of the process step.
delivery: Process window specification document

name: Process sensitivity analysis
definition: A documentation set that quantifies the impact of input variable changes on the variation of process output metrics — establishing sensitivity coefficients for each critical parameter.
purpose: Validates the process window boundaries and confirms process freeze decisions; minimizes the risk of low yield due to uncontrolled process variability.
chars: Sensitivity coefficient tables, parameter ranking by impact, DOE response surface plots.
delivery: Process sensitivity analysis document
comment: Combine process sensitivity analysis with process corner definition into the process window documentation package.

name: Process corner definition method
definition: The methodology for identifying the boundary limits of manufacturing parameter variation — both through simulation (TCAD) and experimental wafer data — that defines the process corners used in device characterization.
purpose: Ensures device operation across the full process variation space and defines safe operating regions; validates TCAD/SPICE compact model accuracy.
chars: 5 primary PVT process corners: TT (Typical-Typical), SS (Slow-Slow), FF (Fast-Fast), SF (Slow-Fast NMOS/PMOS), FS (Fast-Slow NMOS/PMOS).
delivery: TCAD simulation models / DOE characterization documents
comment: Overlaps with process window documentation — consider combining.

name: Yield  leaning
definition: A structured set of actions to detect and reduce wafer defects, prevent emerging failure mechanisms, and progressively increase manufacturing yield.
purpose: Optimizes manufacturing yield efficiency through systematic defect analysis, process improvement, and preventive action implementation.
chars: Documentation sets covering defect inspection and classification, FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis), failure analysis (FA), corrective action reports, and SPC data.
delivery: Yield learning reports and documentation

name: Inline monitoring strategy
definition: A collection of strategies for selecting measurement methods, measurement insertion frequency, and sample sizes for non-destructive in-line measurements performed on production wafers or after process steps.
purpose: Enables early defect detection, process variation control, root-cause analysis, and equipment calibration through systematic data collection.
chars: Measurement of critical dimensions (CD) and physical properties; surface defect inspection methods; sampling plan design.
delivery: Inline monitoring strategy document

name: Split lot design
definition: A structured approach to dividing a production wafer lot into sub-groups to evaluate the effect of a single process or equipment variable while holding all other variables constant.
purpose: Determines optimal target parameter settings, eliminates confounding environmental variables, and reduces experimental cost and cycle time compared to full-factorial DOE.
chars: Split by equipment parameter (e.g., different power settings) or by equipment type (tool A vs. tool B) within the same lot.
delivery: Split lot design plan + analysis report

name: Tool qualification and matching
definition: A documentation set for verifying that a specific process tool is capable of executing the process within specification, and the procedure for calibrating and matching tools of the same type so that wafer output results are consistent regardless of which tool is used.
purpose: Ensures manufacturing flexibility (any tool can run any lot), minimizes inter-tool variation, and stabilizes device electrical parameters across all tools.
chars: Static qualification (measuring tool parameters without wafers), dynamic qualification (processing wafers and measuring results), matching to reference tool fingerprint, and tool fingerprint characterization.
delivery: Tool qualification and matching procedure documents

name: Test structure design
definition: Simplified test structures designed to evaluate one or more specific device or process parameters in isolation from the full device complexity.
purpose: Parameter extraction, process failure mechanism identification within the process flow, process stability monitoring, and DFM rule validation.
chars: Electrical test structures, physical dimension measurement targets, reliability stress structures, and process evaluation patterns.
delivery: Test structure design document / layout GDS drawings

name: Yield ramp-up methodology
definition: A structured methodology and action framework for systematically increasing manufacturing yield from initial process bring-up levels to production-ready targets.
purpose: Provides a repeatable, data-driven approach to achieve and sustain yield improvement milestones during process ramp.
chars: Yield learning cycles, defect reduction priorities, inline monitoring strategy, split-lot experiments, and yield target milestone plan.
delivery: Yield ramp-up methodology document

name: Defect classification
definition: The systematic process of analyzing and categorizing wafer defects detected by inspection tools into defined defect categories.
purpose: Identifies defect source processes, classifies defect severity and impact on device functionality, and improves Automatic Defect Classification (ADC) system accuracy.
chars: Classification by morphology (shape, size), classification by material composition (chemical analysis), classification by process layer and functional impact.
delivery: Defect classification documents and ADC training library

name: SPC
definition: Statistical Process Control (SPC) — a systematic tool for monitoring equipment variables and inline process parameters to detect and respond to process deviations.
purpose: Detects process abnormalities in real time, prevents out-of-specification product, optimizes process control costs, and ensures product consistency across all lots.
chars: SPC control charts (X-bar/R, EWMA, CUSUM) and OCAP (Out of Control Action Plan) integrated into the production information system.
delivery: SPC implementation documents and control chart specifications

name: FDC (Fault detection and classification)
definition: A method and software tool system for automatically detecting whether a fault has occurred during a process run and classifying the fault type by severity and likely source.
purpose: Increases yield by intercepting faulty lots before the fault propagates; reduces equipment downtime by identifying equipment issues early; ensures product quality consistency.
chars:
Sensors & Data Acquisition: Specification of process parameters to be monitored at each equipment step.
Advanced statistical models & algorithms for fault detection (multivariate SPC, PCA).
Classification Engine: Rules for categorizing detected faults by type and severity.
Integration & Response mechanisms: Automated lot hold, alarm escalation, and MES notification.
delivery: FDC configuration documents — engineering drawings

name: Q-time controls
definition: Specifications defining the maximum allowable queue time between two consecutive process steps, and the required actions if the limit is exceeded.
purpose: Prevents device performance degradation or yield loss caused by surface chemical changes, contamination, or material diffusion that occur when wafers wait too long between certain process steps.
chars:
Q-time constraint table (time limit per step-pair)
Action list/escalation plan if Q-time is violated (hold lot, expedite, rework, or scrap decision tree).
delivery: Q-time control specification documents

name: R2R APC
definition: Run-to-Run Advanced Process Control (R2R APC) — an advanced process control method in which equipment process parameters are automatically adjusted between wafer runs based on upstream measurement data or predictive models to maintain process output at target values.
purpose: Minimizes lot-to-lot process variation, maintains high process capability index (Cpk), and reduces out-of-specification product.
chars: R2R models and algorithms → control strategy: Feedback control (adjust based on previous lot results), feedforward control (adjust based on incoming material measurements).
delivery: R2R APC know-how / methodology documents
comment: TTP to advise on which tools and how many tools benefit from R2R APC implementation.

name: Lesson learn
definition: The systematic process of collecting, analyzing, and archiving knowledge gained from both successes and failures during manufacturing operations.
purpose: Prevents recurrence of known failure modes, shortens troubleshooting response time, optimizes maintenance procedures, and accelerates new engineer training.
chars: 8D (Eight Disciplines) problem-solving reports, knowledge management database, FMEA updates triggered by new failure events.
delivery: Knowledge database records and lesson-learned reports

name: FMEA (failure mode and effects analysis)
definition: A systematic engineering analysis method used to proactively identify all possible failure modes of a process or equipment, evaluate their effects and severity, and implement preventive actions before failures occur on production wafers.
purpose: Optimizes yield by preventing known failure mechanisms, minimizes unplanned downtime, and standardizes process risk management.
chars:
Failure Mode: The specific way a process or equipment can fail.
Failure Effect: The impact on downstream product quality or yield.
Potential Causes: Root causes of the failure mode.
RPN (Risk Priority Number) = Severity × Occurrence × Detection.
Control Plan, SOP/WI (Standard Operating Procedure / Work Instruction), OCAP (Out of Control Action Plan).
delivery: FMEA documents and control plan

name: Equipment maintanence IP
definition: Maintenance procedures, predictive failure algorithms, improved component designs, and best-known methods for sustaining equipment performance and reliability.
purpose: Maximizes Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE), extends the lifetime of consumable parts, and maintains process stability by preventing unexpected equipment degradation.
chars: Predictive maintenance models, e-diagnostics and remote monitoring systems, improved spare parts specifications, best-known methods (BKM) for preventive maintenance.
delivery: Maintenance procedure documents and equipment IP specifications
comment: Equipment vendor provides equipment-specific maintenance IP.

name: Defect control strategy
definition: A set of procedures defining inspection frequency, measurement methods, warning thresholds, and response actions when physical defects are detected on wafers.
purpose: Maintains stable yield performance, optimizes inspection cost vs. yield protection trade-off, and manages defect-related product quality risks.
chars: Sampling strategy, defect budget (maximum allowable defect density per layer), automated defect classification (ADC), and defect source segmentation analysis.
delivery: Defect control strategy specification documents

name: Defect root cause library
definition: A systematic database storing SEM/TEM images, morphological identification characteristics, chemical signatures, and confirmed root causes of defect types that have occurred in the manufacturing process.
purpose: Reduces defect investigation response time, standardizes defect classification decisions, and enables long-term yield improvement through systematic knowledge retention.
chars: SEM image library organized by defect type, defect fingerprint maps, EDS/WDS chemical composition database, corrective action handbook.
delivery: Defect root cause library database and reference documentation

name: Cost optimization
definition: A continuous improvement process for reviewing and eliminating manufacturing waste and improving resource utilization efficiency without compromising technical specifications or product reliability standards.
purpose: Increases gross profit margins, optimizes cost structure, improves competitive manufacturing productivity, and sustains supply chain resilience.
chars: OEE improvement initiatives, consumable material reduction, yield optimization, alternative supplier qualification, and energy efficiency programs.
delivery: Cost optimization reports and methodology documents
comment: General consulting-level knowledge; typically provided as advisory input.

name: Capacity planning
definition: The process of determining the required manufacturing capacity of a fab to meet forecasted customer demand across different time horizons.
purpose: Optimizes capital investment allocation, eliminates production bottlenecks, ensures on-time delivery commitments, and enables workforce and supply planning.
chars: Tool-based capacity modeling (equipment utilization and bottleneck analysis), short-term and long-term planning, bottleneck identification and resolution.
delivery: Capacity planning documents and simulation models
comment: General consulting-level knowledge; typically provided as advisory input.

name: Electrical test information
definition: Specifications of measurement methods that determine the electrical operating characteristics of devices (WAT — Wafer Acceptance Test) and validate the functional logic of the chip (CP — Circuit Probe / FT — Final Test).
purpose: Provides electrical characterization data for process control, model calibration, yield learning, and product qualification sign-off.
chars: (WAT/CP/FT) Test conditions/methods/durations, sampling plans, prober/tester recipes, WAT parameter matrix, CP test program for devices/SRAM/IO, final test (FT) where applicable.
delivery: Electrical test specification documents

name: Metrology information
definition: Physical measurement methods for measuring thin film thickness, critical dimensions (CD), and layer-to-layer overlay alignment accuracy.
purpose: Provides process monitoring data for inline process control, model calibration, yield correlation, and equipment qualification.
chars: Inline and offline measurement methods and recipes for: CD-SEM (Critical Dimension Scanning Electron Microscopy), OCD (Optical CD / scatterometry), overlay metrology, film thickness (ellipsometry, XRF), stress, sheet resistance (Rs), XPS, with measurement insertion points vs. process flow.
delivery: Metrology specification documents

name: Defect-inspection information
definition: Detection of physical particles and structural defects on wafers to maintain and improve manufacturing yield through systematic inspection and classification.
purpose: Provides defect density data for yield correlation, process control, contamination source identification, and ADC model training.
chars: Darkfield/Brightfield (DF/BF) optical inspection recipes, e-beam inspection recipes, OEBR inspection recipes, defect binning and classification rules, reticle inspection methodology, defect library and database schema, fab-level and product-level defect reduction methodologies.
delivery: Defect inspection specification documents

name: Correlation data
definition: Established quantitative linkages between manufacturing process step parameters, physical measurement values (from metrology tools), and device electrical characteristics or circuit performance metrics.
purpose: Used for process control decision-making, detection of out-of-specification events, validation of new process modules, and predictive device performance modeling.
chars: Physical/process variable — device property correlated data sets for each manufacturing process step, including regression models and correlation coefficients.
delivery: Correlation data sets and analysis documentation

name: Metrology involvement
definition: The systematic application of measurement science to every step of the semiconductor manufacturing process to collect process feedback data.
purpose: Provides real-time process monitoring data for process maintenance, control, and optimization; supports new process development; enables troubleshooting and product classification.
chars:
Data collected from in-line and off-line metrology tools.
Machine learning inference: feeding process-variable data to ML models.
Predictive output: the model uses known correlations (e.g., how a specific plasma power shift affects etch depth) to estimate film thickness or CD values.
Electrical probing for process-to-device electrical correlation.
delivery: Metrology engagement plan and data collection documentation

name: Measurement system capability
definition: Assessment of measurement tool performance based on statistical standards — commonly referred to as Measurement System Analysis (MSA) or Gauge study.
purpose: Ensures the measurement system provides reliable, consistent, and low-bias data, enabling accurate decisions in statistical process control (SPC) and product disposition.
chars:
Gauge R&R (Gauge Repeatability and Reproducibility) report:
- Repeatability: Variation when the same operator measures the same sample multiple times with the same tool.
- Reproducibility: Variation when different operators or different tools measure the same sample.
Acceptance Criteria (AIAG MSA Standards): Evaluated using Precision-to-Tolerance Ratio (P/T Ratio). P/T < 10% = excellent; 10-30% = acceptable.
delivery: MSA / Gauge R&R study report
comment: Reference: https://www.instron.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/understanding-gage-r-and-r-concepts-and-its-significance-for-instron-systems.pdf

name: Reliability of test infrastructure
definition: The capability of the entire measurement system — hardware, software, environment, and system integration — to maintain consistent measurement performance stably over time and under actual fab operating conditions. Includes ATE, test fixtures (load boards, probe cards), and on-wafer test structures.
purpose: Ensures measurement equipment systems operate accurately and consistently over time and under real manufacturing conditions.
chars:
Stability / drift over time: the same tool produces consistent results for the same sample over time.
Environmental reliability: the system maintains measurement accuracy under fab environmental variations — vibration, temperature fluctuations, humidity, electromagnetic interference.
Tool-to-tool matching / site-to-site consistency across the same tool type.
delivery: Test infrastructure reliability qualification report
comment: Reference: Fundamentals of Semiconductor Manufacturing and Process Control — G. May, C. Spanos; Design Rules in a Semiconductor Foundry — Eitan N. Shauly; Semiconductor Manufacturing Handbook — H. Geng (2018)

name: TDDB lifetime models
definition: Time-Dependent Dielectric Breakdown (TDDB) lifetime models — describing the gradual degradation and eventual breakdown of the gate dielectric under sustained electric field stress, leading to a short circuit between gate and channel.
purpose: Predicts gate oxide reliability lifetime and qualifies the technology node against customer reliability requirements (e.g., 10-year operating life at specified use conditions).
chars:
Thermochemical model (E-model): Assumes breakdown lifetime (TBD) depends exponentially on oxide electric field strength (Eox).
Anode hole injection model (1/E-model): Models anode hole injection (AHI) as the primary degradation mechanism.
Power-law model (PL model): Used for ultra-thin oxide layers (<5 nm) where tunneling dominates.
E^0.5 model: Used for high-k dielectric materials.
delivery: TDDB reliability model files and qualification documentation
comment: Reference: Design Rules in a Semiconductor Foundry — Eitan N. Shauly

name: BTI degradation models
definition: Bias Temperature Instability (BTI) degradation models — per AEC-Q100 standard — describing threshold voltage shift in transistors under sustained gate bias at elevated temperature.
purpose: Predicts transistor parameter shift over operational lifetime and ensures the technology meets automotive and consumer reliability degradation limits.
chars:
NBTI (Negative BTI): Occurs in PMOS when the gate is negatively biased at high temperature. Primary mechanism is Si-H interface bond breaking.
PBTI (Positive BTI): Occurs in NMOS, particularly severe with high-k/metal-gate dielectric materials due to electron trapping in pre-existing dielectric defects.
Power-law model: Threshold voltage shift ΔVth ∝ t^n.
delivery: BTI degradation model files and characterization data
comment: AEC-Q100 standard reference for automotive qualification.

name: HCI models
definition: Hot Carrier Injection (HCI) degradation models — describing the phenomenon where high-energy ('hot') charge carriers in the MOSFET channel gain sufficient energy near the drain to overcome the Si/SiO₂ energy barrier and become injected into the gate oxide, creating interface traps and oxide charge.
purpose: Predicts transistor drive current and threshold voltage degradation due to HCI during product lifetime.
chars:
Lucky Electron Model (LEM): Assumes carrier injection rate is proportional to substrate current (Isub).
Modes: DAHC (Drain Avalanche Hot Carrier), CHC (Channel Hot Carrier).
AC-DC degradation ratio: In real product operation (AC mode), HCI lifetime is typically ~50× longer than DC stress test conditions.
delivery: HCI degradation model files and stress test characterization data

name: EM lifetime prediction
definition: Electromigration (EM) lifetime prediction models — describing the physical displacement of metal atoms driven by electron momentum transfer in current-carrying conductors, creating voids (opens) or hillocks (shorts).
purpose: Predicts metal interconnect failure lifetime and ensures all metal wires are designed within the allowable current density limits for the required product lifetime.
chars:
Black's Equation: Industry-standard model for Mean Time to Failure (MTTF) prediction: MTTF ∝ j⁻ⁿ × exp(Ea/kT).
Immortality criterion: For very short conductor segments, mechanical back-stress can balance the EM driving force, preventing failure (Blech limit).
Material comparison: Copper (Cu) has significantly better EM resistance than aluminum (Al).
delivery: EM lifetime model files and characterization data

name: FA flow decision tree
definition: Systematic measurement methods, analytical tools, and step-by-step decision procedures for electrical fault analysis and physical failure root-cause identification.
purpose: Provides a structured, reproducible approach to identify the physical root cause of device or circuit failures.
chars:
EFA (Electrical Failure Analysis): IV curve tracing, TDR (Time-Domain Reflectometry), thermal emission microscopy.
Failure localization: Emission microscopy, dynamic laser stimulation (DLS), nanoprobe, EBAC (Electron Beam Absorbed Current).
PFA (Physical Failure Analysis): Chemical/laser decapsulation, RIE delayering, FIB (Focused Ion Beam) cross-sectioning.
Root-cause playbooks and decision trees.
Cross-layer defect interpretation methodology.
delivery:
FA procedure documents (SOP, recipe transfer, spec sheets);
Root-cause playbook (decision tree, signature library, expert knowledge system, case studies)

name: Physical root-cause libs
definition: A systematically compiled and classified collection of failure patterns, their physical signatures, and their confirmed root causes — used to cross-reference new failure observations against historical data.
purpose: Enables rapid failure identification through pattern matching against known failure signatures, reducing FA cycle time.
chars: Example: The Parametric Interpretation Expert System (PIES) implemented for the ISO-Z bipolar process at Fairchild Semiconductor contained 342 failure case types: 101 at measurement level, 82 at physical structure level, and additional categories at higher abstraction levels.
delivery: Physical root-cause library database
comment:
Reference: Fundamentals of Semiconductor Manufacturing and Process Control — G. May, C. Spanos.
https://doi.org/10.1609/aimag.v7i4.559

name: Cross-layer defect interpretation
definition: A systematic engineering process that links defect data from multiple manufacturing stages to identify failure root causes spanning multiple process layers and to assess the likelihood of those defects causing functional chip failures.
purpose: Enables root-cause identification for complex multi-layer defects that cannot be attributed to a single process step.
chars:
Common-Unique Analysis: Uses defect attributes (size, brightness, design location) to cross-correlate defects between layers, identifying nuisance defects (Nuisance Filtering) and Defects of Interest (DOI).
Cross-Layer Reliability Modeling: Converts physical failures at lower structural levels into circuit-level or system-level performance impact assessments.
delivery: Cross-layer defect interpretation methodology documents
comment:
Patent: https://patents.google.com/patent/US11151711B2/fr
Reference: https://doi.org/10.3390/jimaging12040142

name: HTOL/ HAST/ TC test flows
definition:
HTOL (High Temperature Operating Life): Simulates real-world operation under extreme conditions (elevated voltage and temperature) to determine useful operating lifetime.
HAST (Highly Accelerated Stress Test): Tests package moisture resistance under combined high-temperature, high-humidity bias stress conditions.
TC (Temperature Cycling): Tests mechanical durability of the package and die against cyclic thermal stress caused by differential thermal expansion.
purpose: Combining these accelerated lifetime models with data from test element groups (TEG) allows foundries to certify chips meet 10-20 year lifetime targets for consumer and automotive applications.
chars:
TTP should demonstrate that the technology/product passes the specified stress conditions, based on JEDEC standard sets (e.g., JEP001-3A, JESD22-A108F, JESD22-A110E, JESD22-A104E) or equivalent standards.
HTOL: Chip is dynamically biased at elevated voltage and 125°C junction temperature for 1000 hours.
HAST: Chip is stressed at 130°C / 85% relative humidity with bias applied for 96 hours minimum.
delivery: HTOL/HAST/TC test flow specification documents and qualification data
comment: Reference: Design Rules in a Semiconductor Foundry — Eitan N. Shauly

name: Acceleration factor derivation
definition: The method for establishing the mathematical relationship between accelerated stress test conditions (temperature, humidity, voltage, etc.) and the failure rate observed, then deriving the acceleration factor (AF) that converts test-time failures to real-use-time failures.
purpose: Extrapolates and predicts device operational lifetime from accelerated stress test data collected in a fraction of the real-world time.
chars:
Voltage and temperature acceleration models based on physical failure mechanisms (Arrhenius equation for temperature, power-law for voltage).
For early life failure rate: The acceleration factor AF is defined as the ratio of expected time to failure (TF) under accelerated conditions vs. normal use conditions.
delivery: Acceleration factor derivation models and qualification documentation
comment: Reference: Design Rules in a Semiconductor Foundry — Eitan N. Shauly

name: Sample size and confidence models
definition: Statistical models that determine the required number of test samples and the confidence level (statistical certainty) of the conclusions drawn from test data.
purpose: Ensures test results have statistical significance and quantifies the risk when making product quality acceptance or rejection decisions.
chars:
Level of confidence (typically 60%, 90%, 95%).
Sample size requirements for each measurement type.
Example: no extrinsic samples observed in a ramped voltage test with 8000 samples and an average gate area of 0.0005 cm² → defect density estimated ≤ ~0.75 cm⁻² with specified confidence level.
delivery: Statistical sampling plan and confidence model documents
comment: Reference: Design Rules in a Semiconductor Foundry — Eitan N. Shauly

name: FIT (Failure in time) rate calculation methods
definition: FIT (Failure In Time) rate = number of failures per 1 billion device-operating-hours (failures per 10⁹ device-hours). This value is derived from accelerated stress test data. Customers evaluate not only the FIT number but also the specific test conditions used, since FIT values depend on the assumed acceleration conditions.
purpose: Provides a standardized, quantitative metric for comparing device reliability across different products and technologies, and for verifying compliance with customer reliability specifications.
chars: FIT rate calculation methodology using: Failure count from stress tests, acceleration factor AF, total device-hours under stress, and confidence level (per JEDEC JESD85).
delivery: FIT rate calculation documentation and reliability data reports

name: Mission profile translation
definition: The process of converting the actual real-world operating conditions of a device in its intended end application into equivalent accelerated stress conditions for reliability analysis and qualification test design.
purpose: Enables design of representative qualification tests that accurately reflect the device's actual field stress conditions throughout its operational lifetime.
chars:
Mission profiles can be based on thermal conditions (temperature cycling profile), electrical conditions (voltage, current, power dissipation), or combined environmental conditions (temperature + humidity + vibration).
Example: ZVEI (German Electrical and Electronic Manufacturers Association) mission profile standards, SAE International Automotive mission profiles.
delivery: Mission profile translation methodology and stress equivalence calculations
comment: Reference: Design Rules in a Semiconductor Foundry — Eitan N. Shauly

name: Automotive/aerospace qualification logic
definition: The qualification framework and standards logic for ensuring semiconductor products meet the rigorous reliability requirements of automotive and aerospace applications (e.g., AEC-Q100 for ICs, ISO 26262 for functional safety).
purpose: Develops a quality management system that provides continuous improvement, with emphasis on defect prevention and minimization of variability-related waste in the supply chain.
chars:
Process and device FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis).
Change notification procedures for process changes and manufacturing abnormality events, with special approval workflows for control plan modifications.
Potential issue identification and impact estimation → preventive activity planning.
delivery: Automotive/aerospace qualification framework documents
comment: Reference: Design Rules in a Semiconductor Foundry — Eitan N. Shauly

name: Automotive Qualification Plan
definition: A structured qualification plan covering all activities required to certify a semiconductor product for automotive use, from design characterization through manufacturing release.
purpose: Demonstrates that the product meets automotive reliability and functional safety requirements across the full production variation range and all specified temperature and voltage operating conditions.
chars:
Characterization plan: Ensures functionality over production variations and all temperature/voltage ranges.
Demonstration of manufacturability: Verifies high reliability across the full process variation space.
Physical reliability plan: Addresses all potential reliability risks based on the device mission profile.
delivery: Automotive qualification plan documentation
comment: Reference: Design Rules in a Semiconductor Foundry — Eitan N. Shauly

name: Layer List and Polarity
definition: Specification of the ordered sequence of mask layers (e.g., quartz substrate, chrome absorber layer sequence) and the polarity definition of each mask (whether the digitized pattern areas are clear or opaque for positive or negative tone photoresist development).
purpose: Defines the material layer stack order on each mask and the exposure polarity convention required to correctly expose the photoresist during wafer lithography.
chars: Layer name and sequence table, polarity definition per mask layer (clear-field vs. dark-field), tone assignment for positive/negative photoresist.
delivery: Layer list and polarity specification document

name: Pellicle/Inspection Plan
definition:
Pellicle material specifications: transparent to DUV lithography wavelengths (>99% transmission), with a standoff distance designed so any particle falling on the pellicle membrane is out of the scanner focal plane and does not print on the wafer.
Mask inspection methodology: die-to-die comparison, die-to-database comparison, aerial image measurement system (AIMS), optical and electron-beam inspection systems.
purpose: Protects masks from particle contamination during production use; defines the periodic inspection schedule for detecting mask defects and the criteria for mask replacement.
chars: Pellicle material specifications, attachment procedures, inspection frequency and acceptance criteria, mask defect disposition rules.
delivery: Pellicle and mask inspection plan document

name: Fracturing and Geometry Algorithms
definition: Algorithms for decomposing curved and complex layout polygon shapes into simple rectilinear or trapezoidal polygons that can be written by variable-shaped electron-beam (e-beam) lithography systems.
purpose: Converts design layout geometry into a format compatible with mask writing tools for physical mask fabrication.
chars: Shot fracturing algorithms, shape-merging logic, proximity effect correction (PEC) during e-beam writing.
delivery: Fracturing software + operational guide
comment: Patent: Method and system for design of a reticle to be manufactured using variable shaped beam lithography

name: ORC (Optical Rule Check)- Mask rule check
definition: Similar to layout DRC, these are algorithms that check whether the mask design meets specific mask manufacturability rules — verifying that all features are manufacturable by the mask-writing system and printable by the lithography scanner.
purpose: Verifies the manufacturability and printability of all features defined in the mask data.
chars: Minimum feature size for e-beam writing, mask critical dimension uniformity, pattern density constraints, and MEEF (Mask Error Enhancement Factor) considerations.
delivery: Mask rule check deck files
comment: Patent: Mask rule checking for curvilinear masks for electronic circuits

name: File format and data stuctures
definition: Standard and proprietary data formats for mask layout data (e.g., GDSII, OASIS) and the associated data structures used by EDA companies to manage mask design data hierarchies.
purpose: Systematic control and organization of mask design data for efficient processing, storage, and transfer between design and mask fabrication systems.
chars: GDSII and OASIS file format specifications, hierarchical cell structure definitions, layer mapping tables.
delivery: Software tools for mask data format handling

name: Mask data translation
definition: The process of converting design-level layout data (GDSII/OASIS) into mask-level data format required by the mask writing tool.
purpose: Converts chip layout design data into the specific format required by mask fabrication equipment for physical mask writing.
chars: Data conversion algorithms, layer mapping during translation, resolution scaling for mask magnification.
delivery: Mask data translation software
comment: Patent: Method for automatic mask tool translation

name: RET/OPC (Resolution Enhancement Tech / Optical Proximity Correction) kits
definition: Mathematical computational lithography techniques for modifying mask shapes to compensate for optical diffraction effects and process proximity effects that distort the printed image on the wafer.
purpose: Makes the lithographically printed image on silicon match the intended design as closely as possible. Example: Adding small sub-resolution 'serif' features to corners of metal lines so the printed corners remain sharp rather than rounding.
chars: Includes OPC (Optical Proximity Correction), SRAF (Sub-Resolution Assist Features), and PSM (Phase Shift Mask) techniques.
delivery: OPC/RET computational lithography software kit and recipe files

name: OPC test-keys
definition: Designed test masks and measurement structures used to test OPC model accuracy, calibrate OPC simulation models, and verify lithographic process window.
purpose: Calibrates and validates OPC models to ensure they accurately predict the printed image; verifies OPC performance across the full range of design patterns.
chars: CD measurement targets, 1D and 2D OPC calibration structures, through-pitch and through-density test patterns.
delivery: Test key layout GDS files + OPC model calibration algorithm
comment: Paper: The new test pattern selection method for OPC model calibration

name: SMO strategies
definition: Source and Mask co-Optimization (SMO) — simultaneously optimizing the illumination source shape and the mask pattern geometry to achieve the best printability using a mathematical cost function.
purpose: Simultaneously optimizes the illumination source and mask to maximize printable feature resolution and image contrast on the wafer.
chars: Joint optimization algorithms for source shape and mask OPC/SRAF, cost function definitions, process window maximization criteria.
delivery: SMO methodology documents and optimized source definition files
comment: Patent: Fast Freeform source and mask co-optimization method

name: SRAF (Sub-Resolution Assist Features) recipes
definition: Model-based or rule-based sub-resolution assist feature (SRAF) placement recipes designed to maximize aerial image contrast (NILS — Normalized Image Log Slope) without printing on the wafer.
purpose: Improves the quality, contrast, and process window of the printed photoresist image by enhancing constructive interference at target features.
chars: SRAF placement rules (minimum spacing, maximum area), model-based SRAF optimization algorithms, SRAF printing check verification.
delivery: SRAF recipe documents and placement algorithm
comment: Patent: Method for determining corrections to features of a mask

name: Phase Shift Masks design
definition: Mask design methodology that introduces phase information by creating 180° phase shifts between adjacent transparent regions of the mask to produce destructive interference at pattern edges, increasing image contrast beyond conventional binary masks.
purpose: Improves the resolution and edge acuity of the printed photoresist image, enabling printing of features smaller than the diffraction limit of conventional binary masks.
chars: Alternating PSM (Alt-PSM), attenuated PSM (Att-PSM, halftone PSM), chromeless phase lithography (CPL) design variants.
delivery: Phase shift mask design specification documents
comment: Patent: Mask blank, Phase-shift mask, and method of manufacturing semiconductor device

name: mask set definition
definition: A complete definition table for the entire set of reticles required for each device variant (e.g., LP — Low Power, HP — High Performance, HPC — High Performance Computing), covering FEOL, MOL, BEOL, and first-level packaging (flip-chip, wirebonding) layers.
purpose: Provides complete mask set information, including which masks are shared across device variants and all available mask configuration options.
chars:
Name/code for each mask
Functional purpose of each mask layer
Lithography system used (ArFi — ArF immersion, ArF dry, KrF, etc.)
Polarity definition (clear-field or dark-field)
Mask quality grade required
OPC/PSM/SRAF application per mask
delivery: Mask set definition document

name: alignment and overlay marks
definition: Special geometric structures (cross, grating, box-in-box patterns) etched or patterned on different layers of the wafer at designated locations in the scribe line.
purpose: Ensures each successive lithography layer is accurately aligned and registered to the previous layer. Misalignment (overlay error) between via and metal layers causes open or short circuits.
chars:
Alignment Marks: Used by the lithography scanner to determine the absolute position and orientation of the wafer.
Overlay Marks: Used to measure the registration error (overlay offset) between two exposed layers. Industry specification: Overlay Error < 2-5 nm for advanced nodes.
delivery: GDSII layout file of standard alignment/overlay marks from the fab, with placement rules specifying required mark locations in the scribe line

name: hotspot library
definition: A methodology for designing and calibrating overlay error measurement using features on the mask set, enabling accurate determination of whether the photoresist pattern is correctly aligned to the previous layer.
purpose: Provides a reference for inter-layer calibration of registration errors between consecutive photolithography exposures.
chars: Overlay target design library, calibration algorithms, overlay map analysis methods.
delivery: Hotspot library documents
comment: Patent: Overlay mark and method of making

name: hotspot detection method
definition: Methods for identifying layout areas where the process window (range of acceptable dose and focus) is too narrow to maintain yield. Checks for pattern pinching/bridging at defocus, evaluates mask error enhancement factor (MEEF), and detects unintended SRAF printing.
purpose: Detects and reports lithographic hotspot patterns in the layout design that are at risk of printing defects.
chars: Process window analysis tools, pinching/bridging simulation at worst-case focus/dose, MEEF calculation per feature type, SRAF printing verification.
delivery: Hotspot detection software + specification documents

name: Mask quality control
definition: A system of standards for evaluating mask dimensional accuracy, registration accuracy, and mask durability over its operational lifetime in the fab.
purpose: Monitors and controls mask quality degradation over time — including the haze phenomenon (progressive mask surface contamination) — to maintain yield and lithography performance.
chars:
CD Uniformity: Uniformity of critical dimensions across the full mask area (3σ specification).
Registration: Positional accuracy of all features on the mask relative to the nominal design grid.
Pellicle Inspection: Periodic inspection of the protective pellicle membrane for particle contamination or damage.
delivery: Certificate of Conformance (CoC) from the mask shop; mask quality control records
comment: Patent: Method of determining characteristic of patterning process based on defect for reducing hotspot

name: Multi-beam datapath
definition: The data processing pipeline that converts mask layout geometry into a pixelated dose grid, compresses and decompresses this pixel grid data for efficient transfer, and drives multi-beam electron-beam writer systems to fabricate complex curvilinear mask patterns.
purpose: Optimizes mask pattern writing throughput by enabling massively parallel multi-beam writing, reducing mask write time for complex advanced-node patterns.
