chars: Data pixelation and compression algorithms, GPU-accelerated processing pipeline, multi-beam dose assignment and correction.
delivery: Multi-beam datapath software + algorithm documentation

name: Product-specific yield learning
definition: Systematic yield improvement activities targeting failure mechanisms specific to individual customer product designs and layouts.
purpose: Identifies and resolves layout-dependent yield limiters unique to each customer product that are not captured by generic process yield learning.
chars:
Design-sensitive failure mechanism analysis
Layout-dependent yield loss pattern identification
Product yield fingerprinting (yield signature unique to each product layout)
delivery: Yield learning reports specific to customer product design
comment: Patent: Data-conversion method for a multibeam laser writer for very complex microlithographic patterns

name: Design-process interaction
definition: Analysis and management of the interactions between circuit layout design decisions (pattern geometry, density, placement) and manufacturing process variability that affect yield.
purpose: Enables co-optimization of design rules and process parameters to minimize layout-induced yield loss.
chars:
Weak pattern library (patterns vulnerable to process variation)
Hotspot classification by failure mechanism
Layout feedback loop to the DFM-PDK team for design rule improvement
delivery: Design-process interaction analysis reports and feedback documentation

name: Customer ramp playbook
definition: A structured, product-specific guide for executing yield ramp from pilot production to high-volume manufacturing (HVM) for each customer product design.
purpose: Provides a clear, executable plan for achieving and maintaining the yield and quality targets required for each customer's product at HVM volumes.
chars:
- Specification / acceptance limits for each customer product
- Process optimization plan aligned with customer shipment schedule
- Yield ramp-up playbook with action sequence
- Volume readiness criteria (yield target, defect density, Cpk)
- Risk assessment framework and mitigation plans
delivery: Customer ramp playbook document

name: Electrical test
definition: Electrical test programs and methodologies for verifying device and chip electrical functionality at wafer sort (CP) and final test (FT) stages.
purpose: Accurately separates functional and defective die; ensures product shipped to customer meets all electrical specifications.
chars:
Test coverage strategies
Guardbanding rules (setting test limits tighter than spec to account for test system accuracy)
Wafer sort vs. final test correlation analysis
Bin optimization — products passing test but unable to operate at full rated performance (risk parts) require special disposition: classification, additional verification, segregated shipment.
delivery: Electrical test program files and specification documents

name: FDC rules
definition: Fault Detection and Classification (FDC) Rules — a library of statistical models, threshold limits, and pre-configured multivariate sensor configurations for each process tool, defining normal operating state envelopes and fault signature patterns (e.g., abnormal RF power, abnormal gas flow rate, chamber pressure deviation).
purpose: Automatically detects and classifies equipment faults in real time to intercept affected wafers before further processing, reduce yield loss, and minimize unplanned downtime.
chars:
- Signal selection (which parameters are monitored at each process step)
- Fault detection models (multivariate SPC, PCA-based)
- Threshold values and control limits per sensor
- Chamber-to-chamber offset compensation table
- Context suppression windows (PM windows, chamber conditioning transients)
- Correlation to downstream metrology/yield data
- Rule coverage map (which process steps are covered vs. not covered)
delivery: FDC configuration document / know-how

name: Dispatch Logic / Scheduling Rules
definition: A set of prioritization algorithms and decision rules governing which lot will be processed at a given tool at any given time — including hot lot priority escalation, queue time constraint enforcement, equipment dedication rules, and re-entrant flow management.
purpose: Maximizes fab throughput and minimizes wafer cycle time while complying with all process constraints (Q-time, equipment qualification, product mix requirements).
chars:
- Queue Time Rules: Enforces maximum wait time between process steps
- WIP Balance and Bottleneck Logic: Manages WIP distribution across parallel tools
- Tool Qualification Matrix: Rules specifying which lots can run on which tools
- APC-Coupled Dispatch: Integrates APC model state into dispatching decisions
delivery: Dispatch logic specification document / know-how

name: SECS/GEM Configuration
definition: Per-equipment configuration files implementing the SEMI Equipment Communication Standard (SECS/GEM — SEMI E30/E37/E87/E90/E94) communication interface between each process tool and the MES/FDC/APC host servers. Files define collection event definitions, alarm definitions, data variable lists (DVL), and remote command sequences.
purpose: Enables the MES to communicate with, monitor, and control manufacturing equipment — enabling automated lot tracking, recipe download, alarm management, and real-time data collection.
chars:
- Collection Event (CEID) subscription list
- Data Variable (DVVAL) selection per collection event
- Event-to-MES transaction mapping
- Equipment State (SEMI E10) model mapping per tool type
- Alarm configuration and automated lot hold mapping
delivery: SECS/GEM configuration package per tool family, including: GEM capability enablement matrix, CEID subscription lists, DVVAL per CEID, event-to-MES transaction mapping, alarm-to-hold mapping.
comment:
- CEID examples: ProcessJobStarted, WaferProcessingCompleted
- DVVAL examples: RecipeID, WaferID, LotID, ChamberPressure_setpoint, ChamberPressure_actual, FilmThickness_measured

name: RMS Structure
definition: Recipe Management System (RMS) structure — the data model, governance rules, and workflow defining the complete lifecycle for creating, versioning, reviewing, approving, and downloading process recipes to manufacturing equipment.
purpose: Prevents unauthorized recipe modifications; ensures full recipe change traceability and auditability (identifying whether any process change has affected yield or device performance).
chars:
- Recipe Hierarchy: MR (Master Recipe), ER (Equipment Recipe), CR (Chamber Recipe), AMR (APC-Modified Recipe), QR (Qualification Recipe)
- Version numbering schema
- Change control workflow with APC/FDC notification triggers
- Recipe-to-equipment qualification matrix
- RBAC structure for recipe access control
- APC offset architecture
- Golden recipe definitions
- Recipe transfer and comparison protocol
delivery: RMS specification document, including: recipe hierarchy definition, version control schema, change control workflow with APC/FDC notification linkage, recipe-to-equipment qualification matrix, RBAC structure.
comment:
Recipe Hierarchy: MR-Master Recipe, ER-Equipment Recipe, CR-Chamber Recipe, AMR-APC-Modified Recipe, QR-Qualification Recipe
Change control: recipe change → new recipe approval → qualification run → performance verification → release

name: AMHS/MCS Routing Algorithms
definition: The Material Control System (MCS) coordination logic that determines how FOUPs (Front-Opening Unified Pods) are routed through the OHT (Overhead Hoist Transport) rail network — including interbay/intrabay path selection, stocker assignment, hot lot express routing, and traffic congestion avoidance.
purpose: Minimizes wafer transport time, prevents AMHS traffic deadlocks, and ensures hot lots receive priority handling through the fab material flow system.
chars:
- Segment travel time calibration tables
- Multi-objective routing weight calibration
- Deadlock prevention logic
- Stocker input selection logic
- Anticipatory carrier pre-positioning
- Interbay/intrabay handoff logic
- MES-MCS ETA feedback loop
- Queue time urgency routing prioritization
delivery: AMHS/MCS routing algorithm specification document

name: OPC-UA Namespace
definition: Defines the OPC Unified Architecture (OPC-UA) address space for servers deployed on fab process tools and edge controllers — specifying process variable nodes, alarm nodes, and event nodes, along with node IDs, data types, and access permission settings.
purpose: Enables standardized, vendor-neutral data collection from manufacturing equipment into MES, FDC, data lake, and analytics systems.
chars:
- Fab process information model namespace definition
- Cross-system semantic linkage design
- Unified fab integration namespace for interoperability
delivery: OPC-UA namespace specification document

name: Server Configuration
definition: IT server infrastructure configuration specifications for fab manufacturing systems — covering hardware specifications, OS configuration, network settings, high-availability architecture, and software deployment parameters for MES, APC, FDC, YMS, and data management servers.
purpose: Ensures stable, high-availability operation of all critical manufacturing IT systems supporting fab production.
chars: Server hardware specifications, OS hardening configuration, network topology and IP addressing, high-availability and failover configuration, backup schedule.
delivery: Server configuration specification documents

name: WIP Tracking
definition: A relational database schema linking each manufacturing lot → wafer → reticle → equipment run → process step. Includes timestamps, operator ID, equipment ID, recipe version, and measurement results at each step.
purpose: Enables complete lot and wafer traceability for root-cause analysis of yield excursions — helps engineers quickly identify which equipment, shift, or process step caused a yield degradation event.
chars:
- Process flow definition and routing logic
- Carrier and slot-level tracking
- Queue time tracking and enforcement logging
- WIP visibility and cycle time prediction
- Excursion and yield event WIP management (hold, disposition, rework)
delivery: WIP tracking schema specification document

name: Lot Genealogy Schema
definition: A data model defining how the complete genealogical relationships between all manufacturing entities (lot, wafer, reticle, equipment, chamber, recipe version, operator) are recorded, linked, and made queryable.
purpose: Enables complete product traceability across all manufacturing entities, supporting yield excursion root-cause analysis, product recall management, and customer quality reporting.
chars:
- Chamber-level and equipment condition-state extensions
- Consumable material tracking extensions (e.g., gas cylinder lot, photoresist lot)
- Reticle genealogy extensions (mask usage tracking)
- Split/merge/rework lot schema design
- Cross-system linkage schema (APC / FDC / metrology / yield correlation)
- Query index and performance optimization design
delivery: Lot genealogy schema specification document

name: YMS Data Integration Schema
definition: Data pipeline architecture and database schema connecting inline physical measurements (CD-SEM, overlay, film thickness), WAT/CP electrical test data, defect inspection data, and process trace data into the Yield Management System (YMS).
purpose: Enables cross-domain yield correlation analysis (e.g., linking photolithography CD deviation to transistor electrical parameters) for yield improvement insights.
chars:
- Source system interface contracts (MES, metrology tools, inspection tools, electrical testers)
- Yield decomposition framework
- Spatial yield correlation schema (die-level, wafer-level, lot-level)
- Equipment signature correlation model
- Inline-to-final yield prediction schema
- Yield learning velocity and yield detractor tracking
delivery: YMS data integration schema specification document

name: SEM/TEM Image Metadata Schema
definition: A database schema for tagging and indexing SEM/TEM defect and cross-section images along with full manufacturing context: wafer ID, lot ID, process layer, defect coordinates, inspection tool ID, operator, and analysis results.
purpose: Enables systematic, searchable defect image analysis; supports Automatic Defect Classification (ADC) model training; and supports AI/ML model development for defect recognition.
chars:
- Manufacturing context linkage schema (lot, wafer, layer, step)
- Defect classification taxonomy
- Yield correlation linkage schema
- CD-SEM APC feed interface schema
- TEM-specific metadata schema extensions (cross-section location, sample preparation method)
delivery: SEM/TEM image metadata schema specification document

name: Virtual Metrology Models
definition: Statistical or machine learning (ML) models that predict physical wafer measurement outcomes (e.g., film thickness, critical dimension CD, etch depth) from upstream process trace data — such as equipment sensor signals and recipe parameters — without requiring a physical measurement on every wafer.
purpose: Achieves effective 100% wafer measurement coverage for critical process parameters without increasing metrology tool capacity. Enables APC Run-to-Run feedback and provides early warning of potential process drift between scheduled physical measurement points.
chars:
- Model architecture selection per process step (linear regression, neural network, ensemble methods)
- Feature engineering per process step (sensor selection, data preprocessing)
- Training dataset specification and labeling strategy
- Model calibration and validation specifications
- Model reliability index design (confidence scoring)
- VM-to-APC integration interface
- Per-step virtual metrology model library
delivery: Virtual metrology model specification document and model files

name: MLOps Pipeline Configuration
definition: End-to-end workflow infrastructure (MLOps — Machine Learning Operations) for developing, validating, deploying, monitoring, and retraining machine learning models used in fab automation.
purpose: Ensures AI/ML models (FDC classifier, virtual metrology, yield prediction) maintain their accuracy over time as process maturity evolves and process drift occurs.
chars:
- Retraining trigger logic (performance drift detection thresholds)
- Champion/challenger governance framework (controlled model replacement workflow)
- Model versioning and auditability
- Multi-model interaction management (preventing conflicting model recommendations)
- Regulatory compliance and manufacturing change control integration
delivery: MLOps pipeline configuration specification document

name: RBAC Configuration
definition: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) permission matrix specifying which user roles (operator, process engineer, equipment engineer, system administrator, etc.) have read / write / execute access permissions to systems, data tables, recipe libraries, and configuration files across MES, APC, YMS, FDC, and IT infrastructure.
purpose: Prevents unauthorized modifications to critical manufacturing data and recipe parameters; protects confidential yield performance data; ensures regulatory compliance.
chars:
- Recipe management RBAC matrix (who can create, approve, and download recipes)
- Yield-critical action controls (who can release lots from hold, approve process changes)
delivery: RBAC configuration specification document

name: IT/OT Segmentation Rules
definition: Network architecture rules defining the security boundary between the enterprise IT network and the manufacturing OT (Operational Technology) network, specifying allowed communication paths, firewall rules, and data transfer controls.
purpose: Prevents cybersecurity threats from propagating from the enterprise IT network into the manufacturing OT control network, protecting fab production systems.
chars:
- SECS/GEM HSMS session persistence rule
- EDA (Equipment Data Acquisition) bandwidth rate limiting
- MCS directional enforcement and QoS (Quality of Service) settings
- Vendor remote access controls and session monitoring
- Production control system isolation rules
- Inter-zone data classification and transfer controls
delivery: IT/OT segmentation network architecture document

name: IEC 62443 / Cybersecurity Layer
definition: Documented security controls, audit evidence, risk assessment results, and audit procedures demonstrating compliance with the IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity standard — covering OT network asset inventory, vulnerability management, patch management, incident response, and security monitoring.
purpose: Protects fab automation infrastructure from cybersecurity threats; meets customer and regulatory cybersecurity requirements; reduces operational risk from cyber incidents.
chars:
- Risk assessment results and residual risk register
- Semiconductor OT patch management adaptation procedures
- Vendor remote access security controls and monitoring
- Removable media policy and exception list
delivery: IEC 62443 compliance documentation package

name: Data Retention & Archival Policy
definition: Procedural rules specifying how long each data type (process trace data, WAT/CT test results, inline measurement data, SEM images, FDC alarms, MES event logs) is retained, at which storage tier (hot / warm / cold), in which format, and under which access controls — along with data deletion and anonymization procedures.
purpose: Ensures compliance with customer data retention requirements (typically >10 years for automotive); enables long-term yield trend analysis; manages tiered storage costs; prevents loss of historical data needed for failure analysis or legal disputes.
chars:
- Trace data summarization rules (full-resolution vs. summary retention by age)
- Full-resolution retention policy for FDC-flagged and yield excursion lots
- Joint queryability requirement across storage tiers
- IP-sensitive data handling requirements (e.g., virtual metrology training datasets)
delivery: Data retention and archival policy document

name: Backup & Disaster Recovery Procedures
definition: Documented and periodically tested procedures for backing up all critical fab IT systems (MES database, APC models, recipe library, YMS data, RBAC configurations) and restoring them within the defined RTO (Recovery Time Objective) and RPO (Recovery Point Objective).
purpose: Minimizes fab system downtime due to hardware failures, software faults, or cybersecurity incidents — ensuring rapid recovery of manufacturing production.
chars: Backup frequency and scope per system type, recovery testing schedule, RTO/RPO definitions per system criticality tier, off-site backup storage requirements.
delivery: Backup and disaster recovery procedure document

name: Device characterization plan
definition: Structured plan defining the electrical device characterization required to extract baseline transistor behavior, covering I–V, C–V, threshold voltage (Vt), saturation/linear drive current (Idsat, Idlin), subthreshold swing (SS), DIBL, and Pelgrom mismatch.
purpose: Establishes a standardized measurement set to qualify device electrical behavior against target specifications and feed model extraction.
chars: Test list per device type, bias conditions, structure mapping, statistical sampling for mismatch. Aligned to JESD28-A.
delivery: Device characterization plan document + measurement matrix
comment: Must Transfer · JESD28-A

name: Technology qualification plan (TQP)
definition: Plan defining the test vehicle, qualification lot count, sample size, and pass/fail reporting structure used to qualify the technology before pilot/HVM.
purpose: Provides the formal gating framework that determines whether the technology is qualified for the next phase.
chars: Test vehicle definition, qualification lot count, sample-size tables, pass/fail criteria and report template.
delivery: TQP document + qualification report template
comment: Must Transfer

name: Material selection table → BOM
definition: Bill-of-materials derived from the material selection table: the consolidated list of qualified materials (precursors, gases, targets, resists, slurries, wafers) bound to each process module.
purpose: Locks the qualified material set into a controlled BOM for procurement and process reproducibility.
chars: Material-to-module mapping, qualified supplier list, spec linkage, revision control.
delivery: BOM spreadsheet / controlled document
comment: Must Transfer

name: EM/XTEM atlas with metrology acceptance windows
definition: Reference atlas of plan-view and cross-section electron-microscopy (SEM/XTEM) images for each critical structure, annotated with metrology acceptance windows (dimensional tolerances).
purpose: Gives engineers a visual + quantitative reference to judge structural conformance during process development and FA.
chars: Annotated EM/XTEM image library, target dimensions, upper/lower acceptance limits per layer/module.
delivery: Image atlas + acceptance-window table
comment: Must Transfer

name: Raw material / Wafer substrate spec
definition: Specification defining the incoming raw materials and starting wafer substrate (resistivity, orientation, doping, flatness, defect density, epi spec).
purpose: Ensures incoming material consistency as the foundation for reproducible process results.
chars: Substrate type, resistivity range, crystal orientation, TTV/bow/warp, particle and defect limits, epi parameters.
delivery: Wafer/raw-material specification document
comment: Must Transfer

name: Multi-Project Wafer (MPW) policy
definition: Policy governing how multiple customer/internal designs share a single mask set and wafer run during the pilot/shuttle phase.
purpose: Reduces prototyping cost and accelerates design validation by sharing reticle and wafer resources.
chars: Shuttle scheduling, die-area allocation, mask-sharing rules, sign-off and turnaround terms.
delivery: MPW policy / shuttle program document
comment: Must Transfer

name: R&D lot and Engineering lot procedure
definition: Procedure defining how R&D lots and Engineering lots are created, routed, prioritized, and dispositioned, distinct from production lots.
purpose: Provides controlled experimental vehicles for process learning without disrupting production flow.
chars: Lot-type definitions, priority rules, split handling, hold/disposition, traceability requirements.
delivery: Lot procedure / WIP routing document
comment: Must Transfer

name: Process FMEA (PFMEA) template with ranked RPN table
definition: Standardized Process FMEA template capturing failure modes, effects, causes, controls, and a ranked Risk Priority Number (RPN = Severity × Occurrence × Detection) table for prioritized mitigation.
purpose: Systematically prioritizes process risks and drives corrective/preventive action in volume manufacturing.
chars: Severity/Occurrence/Detection scales, RPN ranking, action tracking, re-evaluation columns.
delivery: PFMEA template (ranked RPN table)
comment: Must Transfer

name: Pelgrom / matching coefficient extraction methodology
definition: Methodology for extracting device mismatch (Pelgrom) coefficients (A_Vt, A_β) from measured paired-device data for use in statistical/mismatch models.
purpose: Quantifies random device mismatch for accurate analog/mixed-signal Monte-Carlo and corner modeling.
chars: Paired-structure layout, σ vs 1/√(WL) regression, A_Vt/A_β extraction, area scaling. Aligned to JESD28-A.
delivery: Mismatch extraction methodology + coefficient table
comment: Must Transfer · JESD28-A

name: Structured qualification procedures (PQ gate / PDQ / qual plans)
definition: The structured qualification procedures: transistor PQ gate criteria, process qualification report (PDQ) structure, and product qualification plans (AEC-Q100 for automotive, JEDEC for consumer).
purpose: Defines the formal gates and report structures that certify transistor, process, and product qualification.
chars: PQ gate criteria, PDQ report sections, AEC-Q100 and JEDEC qual-plan templates.
delivery: Qualification procedure pack + report templates
comment: Must Transfer

name: WLR test key design & measurement protocol
definition: Wafer-Level Reliability test-key design and the associated measurement protocol (TDDB, EM, HCI, BTI test structures measured at wafer level).
purpose: Enables fast in-line reliability feedback before package-level qualification.
chars: Test-key structures, stress conditions, measurement sequence, extrapolation to use conditions.
delivery: WLR test-key set + measurement protocol
comment: Must Transfer

name: Customer sign-off protocols
definition: Protocols defining the documentation, data package, and approval steps required for customer sign-off on qualification and release.
purpose: Aligns fab and customer on acceptance evidence and formal release authority.
chars: Data package contents, review checklist, approval matrix, escalation path.
delivery: Customer sign-off protocol document
comment: Must Transfer

name: Stress-test-driven IC qualification flow (JESD47)
definition: Qualification flow for ICs driven by stress tests: qualification flow chart, test sequence, and sample-size tables per JESD47.
purpose: Provides the end-to-end stress qualification flow that certifies IC reliability for release.
chars: Flow chart, test sequence (HTOL/HAST/TC/etc.), sample-size and acceptance tables. Aligned to JESD47.
delivery: Must Transfer · JESD47
comment: Must Transfer

name: Process change qualification (PCQ) procedure
definition: Procedure defining when a process change triggers re-qualification and which subset of qualification tests applies (delta-qualification logic).
purpose: Controls requalification scope so process changes are validated without full re-qual overhead.
chars: Change-classification matrix, triggered-test mapping, sample size, sign-off. Aligned to JESD47.
delivery: Must Transfer · JESD47
comment: Must Transfer

name: Qualification lot traveler / routing definition
definition: Definition of the lot traveler and routing for qualification wafers versus production wafers, ensuring qualification material is uniquely identified and routed.
purpose: Guarantees traceability and correct handling of qualification lots distinct from production.
chars: Traveler fields, routing steps, hold points, identification and segregation rules. Aligned to JESD47.
delivery: Qualification lot traveler / routing spec
comment: Must Transfer · JESD47
